meta.name: 10 Mile Performance
meta.subtitle: Break 75 minutes — sub-4:40/km for 16km
meta.description: An elite-targeted 20-week programme for competitive runners chasing sub-75 minutes for 10 miles. Six running days, high weekly volume, two gym sessions.
meta.coach_notes: Sub-75 for 10 miles requires 4:40/km. The volume in this plan is high — 80+ km at peak. Every easy day must be genuinely easy.

weeks.title: Base establishment
weeks.note: Opening week. Establish structure.

sessions.det: 10km easy · 5:45–6:10/km — The first session of your plan. Run at a comfortable, conversational pace — slower than you think you need to. Building the habit matters more than the pace right now.

2km WU · 8x800m @ 10mi pace (90sec rec) · 2km CD · 12km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
12km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

2km WU · 25min @ threshold (4:30–4:40/km) · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Goblet squat 2x12 · Romanian deadlift 2x12 · Reverse lunge 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first lower body strength session. Keep loads light — this week is about learning the movements. Lower body strength is the foundation of running economy.
12km easy · 8x100m strides — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
18km easy · 5:55–6:15/km — The first long run sets your aerobic foundation. Keep the pace genuinely easy — you should be able to hold a conversation throughout. Finishing comfortably is the goal.

weeks.title: VO2max introduction
weeks.note: First VO2max session builds the aerobic ceiling.

sessions.det: 11km easy · 5:45–6:10/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 6x1km @ 3:55–4:05/km (3min rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
12km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 2x14min @ threshold (90sec rec) · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.
13km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
20km easy — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Volume build
weeks.note: Long run reaches 22km.

sessions.det: 12km easy · 5:45–6:10/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 7x1km @ 3:55–4:05/km (3min rec) · 2km CD — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.
13km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

2km WU · 30min continuous @ threshold · 2km CD — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.
14km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
22km easy — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Deload week
weeks.note: Deliberate recovery week. VO2max adaptations consolidate now.

sessions.det: 6km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
6km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

5km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
6km easy — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.
14km easy — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

weeks.title: Race simulation
weeks.note: 10km time trial at race pace.

sessions.det: 11km easy · 5:45–6:10/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 10km @ 4:40/km (race pace) · 2km CD — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.
12km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · 2x20min @ threshold (2min rec) · 2km CD — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.
13km easy · 8x100m strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

20km easy · last 5km race pace — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

weeks.title: Race week
weeks.note: Race week. The fitness is built.

sessions.det: 3km easy · Strides — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
3km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km easy — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
2km WU · Strides · Race: through 5 miles at 4:40/km, build last 2 miles · Trust the training — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Porridge with banana or toast with peanut butter. 500ml water.
200ml water. Warm up well with strides.
Water at every station. Gel at miles 6 and 10.
Water immediately. Banana or protein snack. Full meal within 90 minutes.
Proper meal — protein, carbohydrates, whatever you want. Well earned.
